dimensions.name: Stakeholder Coverage
dimensions.pass_criteria: The FAQ includes hard questions from at least three stakeholder perspectives: engineering (feasibility, scale, dependencies), business/leadership (why now, why us, success metrics, business model), and legal/compliance (if applicable to the domain). A stakeholder from each group would feel their concerns were taken seriously.
dimensions.fail_criteria: One or more stakeholder perspectives are entirely absent or represented only by softball questions. Engineering questions that only cover happy-path implementation are insufficient — risk and failure modes must be covered.

dimensions.name: Answer Completeness
dimensions.pass_criteria: Every question has either a substantive answer or is explicitly marked [OPEN — owner: X] with a named owner. Open items are genuinely open — not avoidance of a question that has an answer.
dimensions.fail_criteria: One or more questions are unanswered without [OPEN] marking. Or [OPEN] items have no named owner (just 'TBD', 'the team', or blank).

dimensions.name: Blocker Identification
dimensions.pass_criteria: Any open item that would prevent the build from proceeding — legal risk, missing capability, unresolved dependency, compliance requirement — is flagged as [BLOCKER — owner: X]. The engineering team can read this FAQ and know exactly what must be resolved before work begins.
dimensions.fail_criteria: Build-blocking issues are buried in [OPEN] items without distinction, or are not surfaced at all. The engineering team would have to infer blockers rather than having them made explicit.

dimensions.name: No Evasive Answers
dimensions.pass_criteria: Answers to hard questions are direct. When the answer is 'we don't know yet', it is marked [OPEN] rather than dressed up with confident-sounding language that obscures the uncertainty.
dimensions.fail_criteria: Hard questions (e.g. 'What's the business model?', 'What are the legal risks?') receive vague, aspirational, or deflecting answers rather than honest responses or [OPEN] flags.
